articles.author: Nell Clark, Carley Petesch, Curtis Yee
articles.description: NASA's Artemis II is fully fueled ahead of NASA’s planned rocket launch to the moon. The launch is timed to take off Wednesday evening. Watch live with APNews.
articles.title: Live updates: Artemis II fully fueled for NASA's historic return to the moon

articles.author: Nell Clark, Carley Petesch, Curtis Yee, Peter Orsi
articles.description: NASA launches four astronauts to the moon on humanity's first lunar voyage in 53 years.
articles.title: Live updates: Rocket lifts off with four Artemis II astronauts on a mission to the moon and back

articles.author: Nell Clark, Carley Petesch, Curtis Yee, Peter Orsi
articles.description: NASA launches four astronauts to the moon on humanity's first lunar voyage in 53 years.
articles.title: Live updates: Rocket lifts off with four Artemis II astronauts on a mission to the moon and back

articles.author: Fatima Hussein, Mark Sherman
articles.description: President Donald Trump has attended oral arguments at the U.S. Supreme Court, marking a first for a sitting president. On Wednesday, he quietly entered the chambers and sat in the front row, flanked by cabinet members. This move breaks with tradition, as presidents typically maintain distance from the court to avoid influencing decisions. Trump’s presence highlights the high stakes of a case on birthright citizenship, a key issue for his administration. Legal experts say his attendance is unlikely to sway the justices, who pride themselves on independence. Trump's appearance adds a unique dynamic to the proceedings.
articles.title: Trump takes a front-row seat for oral arguments at Supreme Court

articles.author: Mark Sherman
articles.description: The Supreme Court is taking up one of the term’s most consequential cases, President Donald Trump’s order on birthright citizenship declaring that children born to parents who are in the United States illegally or temporarily are not American citizens. The White House says Trump will be in attendance. In arguments Wednesday, the justices will hear Trump’s appeal of a lower-court ruling from New Hampshire that struck down the citizenship restrictions, one of several courts that have blocked them. They have not taken effect anywhere in the country. A definitive ruling is expected by early summer. The White House says Trump will be in attendance Wednesday. The birthright citizenship order is part of his Republican administration’s broad immigration crackdown.
articles.title: Birthright citizenship case argued before Supreme Court

articles.author: David Cohen
articles.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said the military could end its Iran offensive in two to three weeks and will shift responsibility for the Strait of Hormuz to countries that rely on it for oil and shipping.
articles.title: Live updates: Trump estimates US will be done in Iran in 2 to 3 weeks

articles.author: Kevin Freking, Stephen Groves
articles.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson and Senate Majority Leader John Thune have announced what they're calling a path forward to fully funding the Department of Homeland Security and ending a record partial government shutdown. They said in a joint statement that “in the coming days,” Republicans in the House and Senate would follow through on President Donald Trump’s directive to fully fund the department, using two parallel tracks. Neither outcome is guaranteed, and the strategy certain to face opposition from Democrats, if not the GOP’s own ranks.
articles.title: Republican leaders in Congress will pursue a path to ending DHS shutdown

articles.author: Samya Kullab
articles.description: The American freelance journalist who was kidnapped Tuesday in Iraq was described by friends and colleagues as a determined, gutsy reporter. Shelly Kittleson is a 49-year-old Wisconsin native. She often worked without formal assignments from editors and on a shoestring budget, taking shared taxis to lawless corners of Iraq where militia rule outweighs government control. Kittleson has lived abroad for years and built a respected journalism career across the Middle East. She often embedded herself in local communities, sometimes staying with families rather than in hotels. She vanished after being forced into a car by two men at a busy Baghdad intersection.
articles.title: Reporter kidnapped in Baghdad was known for pursuing gutsy assignments

articles.author: Dee-Ann Durbin
articles.description: Hershey said Wednesday it will use classic recipes for all Reese’s products starting next year. The change comes after the grandson of Reese’s founder criticized the company for shifting to cheaper ingredients. Reese’s Peanut Butter Cups have always been made with real milk chocolate or dark chocolate and peanut butter. But a small portion of Hershey’s and Reese’s products, like mini Easter eggs, are now made with a coating that contains less chocolate. Hershey said that in 2027, it will shift those products to “their classic milk chocolate and dark chocolate recipes.” It will also be transitioning to natural colors in its sweets next year.
articles.title: Hershey says it will shift back to classic recipe for all Reese's products after criticism
